title: NOC 22210 – Architectural Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Architectural Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by residential and commercial construction growth, infrastructure upgrades, green-building projects, and urban redevelopment programs. Classified under NOC 22210 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Prepare CAD drawings, specifications & cost estimates
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year college diploma in architectural technology or related field
Certification through provincial technologist associations (often employer-preferred)
Two years of supervised industry experience before certification
Mandatory regulatory-body membership in British Columbia
In Quebec, regulatory membership required to use the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This competitive market favors candidates with:
additional_information: Architectural & design firmsConstruction contractors & developersMunicipal & provincial governmentsInfrastructure-planning agenciesReal-estate development companiesSustainable-building consultancies
Annual salaries typically range between $38,500–$92,400 CAD, depending on province, certification status, and experience.
